page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-384GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 32 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory. The underlying hardware features an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU configuration consists of 32 physical cores with a thread-per-core ratio of 1.0, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. This server does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. Single-core processing is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the bottom 10% and Geekbench text processing in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core execution is more robust, with stress-ng multi-core performance, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores all landing in the middle 50%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, PassMark CPU extended instructions, and PassMark CPU encryption tests demonstrate strong performance, placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and database operations consistently perform within average parameters.

The primary tradeoff of this server lies in its shared CPU allocation and weak single-core performance, which limits its suitability for latency-sensitive or single-threaded tasks. Conversely, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 12.0 GB per core provides substantial capacity for memory-intensive workloads. Given the shared CPU model, it offers a cost-efficient profile for resource-dense, non-production environments or batch processing. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded data decompression, memory-cached databases, offline software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference prompt processing.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-384GB is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 32 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, running on AMD EPYC 7542 processors via KVM. It utilizes a shared CPU allocation model with a 2.0 GHz clock speed and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate a significant contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities; single-core stress-ng results fall in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark encryption tests rank in the top 25%. Most other metrics, including memory bandwidth, database operations, and LLM inference, perform in the average tier. With 12.0 GB of memory per core, this server is structured for memory-heavy tasks that can tolerate shared CPU resources. It is suited for multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and memory-cached database workloads.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-384GB is a KVM virtual server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 384 GB RAM. It features a shared CPU model, offering strong multi-threaded performance alongside weak single-core metrics.

tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring thirty-two shared vCPUs and three hundred eighty-four gigabytes of RAM for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 32 AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs with shared CPU allocation
384.0 GB of system memory providing 12.0 GB per core
Strong multi-threaded decompression and encryption performance
Weak single-core processing and text processing capabilities
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for memory-cached databases and batch compilation
